lastPrompt: how to stop claude from signing PR descriptions with "Generated with Claude"?

content.text:
Good news — you've **already done it**. The setting that controls this is `includeCoAuthoredBy`, and your claude-code module already sets it:

```nix
# home-manager/claude-code/default.nix:16
includeCoAuthoredBy = false;
```

And I confirmed it's live in your deployed `~/.claude/settings.json`:
```json
"includeCoAuthoredBy": false
```

## What that setting does
`includeCoAuthoredBy = false` suppresses **both** attribution bits that Claude Code would otherwise add — in commits *and* PR descriptions:
- `🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)`
- `Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>`

It works by making Claude Code omit those instructions from the model's system prompt, so they never get generated.

## If you're still seeing it
- **Restart the session.** `settings.json` is read at session start, so a Claude Code session that was already running when the setting landed (it was added Jun 26) still uses the old behavior. New sessions pick it up — and yours is deployed now, so a fresh session won't add it.
- **Belt-and-suspenders (optional):** if a session ever still slips one in, add an explicit line to a `CLAUDE.md` (global `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` or per-repo):
  > Do not add "Generated with Claude Code" or "Co-Authored-By: Claude" to commit messages or PR descriptions.

So there's nothing to change — the config is correct and active. Just make sure the Claude Code session creating the PRs was started after the setting was deployed.

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

content: You were tidying up the dotfiles repo's Neovim and Nix config. Everything's committed and the Claude attribution setting is already correct, so there's no pending action unless you have a new task.

message.content: Currently, the dictation module inside the dev box distro has the functionality to lower the volume of the currently playing music when dictation starts. It keeps that functionality but also introduces the configuration controlled by a feature flag or by a module option to pause the music if it's already started instead of lowering the volume and respectively resume the playback while the dictation is over.

content.text: Let me read the full dictation module to understand the volume logic and option structure.

content.content:
1	# Speech-to-text dictation using whisper.cpp
2	# Usage: dictate (records audio, transcribes with whisper, types result)
3	{
4	  config,
5	  pkgs,
6	  lib,
7	  username,
8	  ...
9	}:
10	
11	with lib;
12	
13	let
14	  cfg = config.services.dictation;
15	
16	  # The whisper binary to use
17	  whisperBin = "${cfg.whisperPackage}/bin/whisper-cli";
18	  whisperServerBin = "${cfg.whisperPackage}/bin/whisper-server";
19	
20	  serverUrl = "http://${cfg.server.host}:${toString cfg.server.port}";
21	
22	  # Absolute model path for the system service ($HOME is not expanded in ExecStart).
23	  serverModelFile = "${
24	    config.users.users.${username}.home
25	  }/.local/share/whisper/ggml-${cfg.model}.bin";
26	
27	  # Transcription step. Sets $RESULT to the trimmed transcription, or exits 1.
28	  # Server mode posts the wav to the always-on whisper-server (model stays
29	  # resident in VRAM); otherwise a one-shot whisper-cli loads the model each run.
30	  transcribe =
31	    if cfg.server.enable then
32	      ''
33	        echo "Transcribing (server)..."
34	
35	        WHISPER_OUTPUT=$(mktemp /tmp/whisper-output-XXXXXX.txt)
36	        WHISPER_ERROR=$(mktemp /tmp/whisper-error-XXXXXX.txt)
37	
38	        HTTP_CODE=$(${pkgs.curl}/bin/curl -s -o "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" -w "%{http_code}" \
39	          --max-time 60 \
40	          -F file=@"$TEMP_WAV" \
41	          -F temperature=0 \
42	          -F response_format=text \
43	          "${serverUrl}/inference" 2>"$WHISPER_ERROR") || true
44	
45	        if [[ "$HTTP_CODE" != "200" ]]; then
46	          echo "ERROR: whisper-server request failed (HTTP ''${HTTP_CODE:-none})!"
47	          echo "Is it running?  systemctl status whisper-server"
48	          echo ""
49	          cat "$WHISPER_ERROR" "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" 2>/dev/null || true
50	          rm -f "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" "$WHISPER_ERROR"
51	          exit 1
52	        fi
53	
54	        RESULT=$(tr -d '\n' < "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//')
55	        rm -f "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" "$WHISPER_ERROR"
56	      ''
57	    else
58	      ''
59	        echo "Transcribing..."
60	
61	        # Run whisper and capture both stdout and stderr
62	        WHISPER_OUTPUT=$(mktemp /tmp/whisper-output-XXXXXX.txt)
63	        WHISPER_ERROR=$(mktemp /tmp/whisper-error-XXXXXX.txt)
64	
65	        if ! ${whisperBin} \
66	          -m "$WHISPER_MODEL" \
67	          -f "$TEMP_WAV" \
68	          -nt \
69	          >"$WHISPER_OUTPUT" 2>"$WHISPER_ERROR"; then
70	
71	          echo "ERROR: Whisper transcription failed!"
72	          echo ""
73	          cat "$WHISPER_ERROR"
74	
75	          rm -f "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" "$WHISPER_ERROR"
76	          exit 1
77	        fi
78	
79	        RESULT=$(grep -v "^\[" "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" | tr -d '\n' | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//')
80	        rm -f "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" "$WHISPER_ERROR"
81	      '';
82	
83	  # Dictation script using whisper.cpp
84	  dictate = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "dictate" ''
85	    set -euo pipefail
86	
87	    DEFAULT_MODEL="${cfg.model}"
88	    DEFAULT_MODEL_PATH="${cfg.modelPath}"
89	    WHISPER_MODEL="''${WHISPER_MODEL:-$DEFAULT_MODEL_PATH}"
90	    TEMP_WAV=$(mktemp /tmp/dictation-XXXXXX.wav)
91	
92	    cleanup() {
93	      rm -f "$TEMP_WAV"
94	    }
95	    trap cleanup EXIT
96	
97	    # Check if model exists, offer to download if not
98	    if [[ ! -f "$WHISPER_MODEL" ]]; then
99	      echo "Whisper model not found at: $WHISPER_MODEL"
100	      echo ""
101	      read -p "Would you like to download the '$DEFAULT_MODEL' model? [y/N] " -n 1 -r
102	      echo ""
103	
104	      if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
105	        echo "Downloading $DEFAULT_MODEL model..."
106	        ${dictate-download-model}/bin/dictate-download-model "$DEFAULT_MODEL"
107	        echo ""
108	        echo "Model downloaded. Starting dictation..."
109	        echo ""
110	      else
111	        echo "Aborted. You can manually download with: dictate-download-model $DEFAULT_MODEL"
112	        exit 1
113	      fi
114	    fi
115	
116	    echo "Recording... Press Enter to stop, Escape to cancel"
117	
118	    # Record audio using sox with a time limit trick for graceful stop
119	    # We'll record to a temp file and use SIGINT for proper buffer flushing
120	    ${pkgs.sox}/bin/rec -q -r 16000 -c 1 "$TEMP_WAV" &
121	    RECORD_PID=$!
122	
123	    # Wait for Enter or Escape key
124	    while true; do
125	      read -r -s -n 1 key
126	      if [[ "$key" == "" ]]; then
127	        # Enter was pressed
128	        break
129	      elif [[ "$key" == $'\e' ]]; then
130	        # Escape was pressed - cancel recording
131	        echo "Cancelled"
132	        kill -INT $RECORD_PID 2>/dev/null || true
133	        wait $RECORD_PID 2>/dev/null || true
134	
135	        # Restore volume if needed
136	        if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-original-volume ]]; then
137	          ORIGINAL_VOL=$(cat /tmp/dictate-original-volume)
138	          ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --set-volume "$ORIGINAL_VOL"
139	          rm -f /tmp/dictate-original-volume
140	        fi
141	
142	        exit 1
143	      fi
144	    done
145	
146	    # Keep recording for 1.5 more seconds to capture end of sentence
147	    echo "Finishing..."
148	    sleep 1.5
149	
150	    # Use SIGINT (like Ctrl+C) for graceful shutdown with buffer flush
151	    kill -INT $RECORD_PID 2>/dev/null || true
152	    wait $RECORD_PID 2>/dev/null || true
153	
154	    # Ensure file is fully written
155	    sync
156	    sleep 0.3
157	
158	    # Restore original volume if it was lowered (for dictate-hotkey)
159	    if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-original-volume ]]; then
160	      ORIGINAL_VOL=$(cat /tmp/dictate-original-volume)
161	      ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --set-volume "$ORIGINAL_VOL"
162	      rm -f /tmp/dictate-original-volume
163	    fi
164	
165	    ${transcribe}
166	
167	    if [[ -n "$RESULT" ]]; then
168	      echo "$RESULT"
169	
170	      # Copy to clipboard (Wayland)
171	      echo -n "$RESULT" | ${pkgs.wl-clipboard}/bin/wl-copy
172	      echo "(copied to clipboard)"
173	    else
174	      echo "No speech detected"
175	      exit 1
176	    fi
177	  '';
178	
179	  # Script to download whisper model
180	  dictate-download-model = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "dictate-download-model" ''
181	    set -euo pipefail
182	
183	    MODEL_DIR="$HOME/.local/share/whisper"
184	    mkdir -p "$MODEL_DIR"
185	
186	    # Available models: tiny, base, small, medium, large, large-v3
187	    MODEL="''${1:-base}"
188	
189	    # Map model names to full filenames
190	    case "$MODEL" in
191	      tiny)    FILE="ggml-tiny.bin" ;;
192	      base)    FILE="ggml-base.bin" ;;
193	      small)   FILE="ggml-small.bin" ;;
194	      medium)  FILE="ggml-medium.bin" ;;
195	      large)   FILE="ggml-large-v3.bin" ;;
196	      large-v3) FILE="ggml-large-v3.bin" ;;
197	      *)       FILE="ggml-$MODEL.bin" ;;
198	    esac
199	
200	    OUTPUT="$MODEL_DIR/ggml-$MODEL.bin"
201	
202	    echo "Downloading whisper model: $MODEL ($FILE)"
203	    echo "This may take a while for larger models..."
204	
205	    ${pkgs.curl}/bin/curl -L --progress-bar -o "$OUTPUT" \
206	      "https://huggingface.co/ggerganov/whisper.cpp/resolve/main/$FILE"
207	
208	    # Verify the download
209	    SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$OUTPUT" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
210	    if [[ "$SIZE" -lt 1000000 ]]; then
211	      echo "ERROR: Download failed or file too small ($SIZE bytes)"
212	      echo "Contents: $(cat "$OUTPUT")"
213	      rm -f "$OUTPUT"
214	      exit 1
215	    fi
216	
217	    echo "Model downloaded to: $OUTPUT ($SIZE bytes)"
218	    echo "Set WHISPER_MODEL env var or use default (base)"
219	  '';
220	
221	  # Wrapper script that runs dictate and handles errors
222	  dictate-wrapper = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "dictate-wrapper" ''
223	    # Run dictate (volume restore happens inside dictate after recording stops)
224	    ${dictate}/bin/dictate
225	    EXIT_CODE=$?
226	
227	    if [[ $EXIT_CODE -eq 0 ]]; then
228	      # Success - signal to hotkey script to paste by creating a temp file
229	      touch /tmp/dictate-success
230	    else
231	      # Error - wait for keypress before closing
232	      echo ""
233	      echo "Press any key to close..."
234	      read -n 1 -s
235	    fi
236	  '';
237	
238	  # Hotkey script for global shortcut
239	  # Mutes audio, opens a floating terminal for dictation, unmutes after
240	  dictate-hotkey = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "dictate-hotkey" ''
241	    # Clean up any previous success marker
242	    rm -f /tmp/dictate-success
243	
244	    # Save current volume and lower to 10% during dictation
245	    ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --get-volume > /tmp/dictate-original-volume
246	    ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --set-volume 10
247	
248	    # Spawn a floating terminal running dictate wrapper
249	    ${pkgs.foot}/bin/foot \
250	      --title="Dictation" \
251	      --window-size-chars=80x15 \
252	      -e ${dictate-wrapper}/bin/dictate-wrapper 2>/dev/null
253	
254	    # After foot closes, type the text if successful
255	    if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-success ]]; then
256	      rm -f /tmp/dictate-success
257	      sleep 0.2
258	      # Get text from clipboard and type it directly (avoids Ctrl+V issues)
259	      ${pkgs.wl-clipboard}/bin/wl-paste --no-newline --type text/plain 2>/dev/null | ${pkgs.wtype}/bin/wtype -d 5 - 2>/dev/null || true
260	    fi
261	  '';
262	
263	in
264	{
265	  # Module options
266	  options.services.dictation = {
267	    enable = mkEnableOption "whisper-based dictation";
268	
269	    model = mkOption {
270	      type = types.enum [
271	        "tiny"
272	        "base"
273	        "small"
274	        "medium"
275	        "large"
276	      ];
277	      default = "base";
278	      description = "Whisper model to use for transcription";
279	    };
280	
281	    modelPath = mkOption {
282	      type = types.str;
283	      default = "$HOME/.local/share/whisper/ggml-${cfg.model}.bin";
284	      description = "Path to the whisper model file";
285	    };
286	
287	    whisperPackage = mkOption {
288	      type = types.package;
289	      default = pkgs.whisper-cpp;
290	      description = "The whisper-cpp package to use for transcription";
291	    };
292	
293	    server = {
294	      enable = mkEnableOption ''
295	        an always-on whisper-server that keeps the model resident in VRAM.
296	        Avoids per-invocation model loads (which can fail under VRAM pressure
297	        from e.g. ollama) and speeds up transcription'';
298	
299	      host = mkOption {
300	        type = types.str;
301	        default = "127.0.0.1";
302	        description = "Host/IP the whisper-server listens on.";
303	      };
304	
305	      port = mkOption {
306	        type = types.port;
307	        default = 8910;
308	        description = "Port the whisper-server listens on.";
309	      };
310	    };
311	  };
312	
313	  # Module implementation
314	  config = mkIf cfg.enable {
315	    # Install dictation tools
316	    users.users.${username}.packages = [
317	      dictate
318	      dictate-download-model
319	      dictate-hotkey
320	      cfg.whisperPackage # Configurable whisper package
321	      pkgs.sox # For audio recording
322	      pkgs.wl-clipboard # For Wayland clipboard
323	      pkgs.pamixer # For volume control
324	      pkgs.foot # Terminal for dictation hotkey
325	      pkgs.wtype # For simulating keypresses (Wayland)
326	    ];
327	
328	    # Always-on whisper-server: loads the model into VRAM once at boot and keeps
329	    # it resident, so dictation never races ollama for a transient allocation.
330	    systemd.services.whisper-server = mkIf cfg.server.enable {
331	      description = "whisper.cpp server (keeps the dictation model resident in VRAM)";
332	      wantedBy = [ "multi-user.target" ];
333	      after = [ "local-fs.target" ];
334	      serviceConfig = {
335	        User = username;
336	        SupplementaryGroups = [
337	          "video"
338	          "render"
339	        ];

file.content:
# Speech-to-text dictation using whisper.cpp
# Usage: dictate (records audio, transcribes with whisper, types result)
{
  config,
  pkgs,
  lib,
  username,
  ...
}:

with lib;

let
  cfg = config.services.dictation;

  # The whisper binary to use
  whisperBin = "${cfg.whisperPackage}/bin/whisper-cli";
  whisperServerBin = "${cfg.whisperPackage}/bin/whisper-server";

  serverUrl = "http://${cfg.server.host}:${toString cfg.server.port}";

  # Absolute model path for the system service ($HOME is not expanded in ExecStart).
  serverModelFile = "${
    config.users.users.${username}.home
  }/.local/share/whisper/ggml-${cfg.model}.bin";

  # Transcription step. Sets $RESULT to the trimmed transcription, or exits 1.
  # Server mode posts the wav to the always-on whisper-server (model stays
  # resident in VRAM); otherwise a one-shot whisper-cli loads the model each run.
  transcribe =
    if cfg.server.enable then
      ''
        echo "Transcribing (server)..."

        WHISPER_OUTPUT=$(mktemp /tmp/whisper-output-XXXXXX.txt)
        WHISPER_ERROR=$(mktemp /tmp/whisper-error-XXXXXX.txt)

        HTTP_CODE=$(${pkgs.curl}/bin/curl -s -o "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" -w "%{http_code}" \
          --max-time 60 \
          -F file=@"$TEMP_WAV" \
          -F temperature=0 \
          -F response_format=text \
          "${serverUrl}/inference" 2>"$WHISPER_ERROR") || true

        if [[ "$HTTP_CODE" != "200" ]]; then
          echo "ERROR: whisper-server request failed (HTTP ''${HTTP_CODE:-none})!"
          echo "Is it running?  systemctl status whisper-server"
          echo ""
          cat "$WHISPER_ERROR" "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" 2>/dev/null || true
          rm -f "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" "$WHISPER_ERROR"
          exit 1
        fi

        RESULT=$(tr -d '\n' < "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//')
        rm -f "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" "$WHISPER_ERROR"
      ''
    else
      ''
        echo "Transcribing..."

        # Run whisper and capture both stdout and stderr
        WHISPER_OUTPUT=$(mktemp /tmp/whisper-output-XXXXXX.txt)
        WHISPER_ERROR=$(mktemp /tmp/whisper-error-XXXXXX.txt)

        if ! ${whisperBin} \
          -m "$WHISPER_MODEL" \
          -f "$TEMP_WAV" \
          -nt \
          >"$WHISPER_OUTPUT" 2>"$WHISPER_ERROR"; then

          echo "ERROR: Whisper transcription failed!"
          echo ""
          cat "$WHISPER_ERROR"

          rm -f "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" "$WHISPER_ERROR"
          exit 1
        fi

        RESULT=$(grep -v "^\[" "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" | tr -d '\n' | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//')
        rm -f "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" "$WHISPER_ERROR"
      '';

  # Dictation script using whisper.cpp
  dictate = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "dictate" ''
    set -euo pipefail

    DEFAULT_MODEL="${cfg.model}"
    DEFAULT_MODEL_PATH="${cfg.modelPath}"
    WHISPER_MODEL="''${WHISPER_MODEL:-$DEFAULT_MODEL_PATH}"
    TEMP_WAV=$(mktemp /tmp/dictation-XXXXXX.wav)

    cleanup() {
      rm -f "$TEMP_WAV"
    }
    trap cleanup EXIT

    # Check if model exists, offer to download if not
    if [[ ! -f "$WHISPER_MODEL" ]]; then
      echo "Whisper model not found at: $WHISPER_MODEL"
      echo ""
      read -p "Would you like to download the '$DEFAULT_MODEL' model? [y/N] " -n 1 -r
      echo ""

      if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
        echo "Downloading $DEFAULT_MODEL model..."
        ${dictate-download-model}/bin/dictate-download-model "$DEFAULT_MODEL"
        echo ""
        echo "Model downloaded. Starting dictation..."
        echo ""
      else
        echo "Aborted. You can manually download with: dictate-download-model $DEFAULT_MODEL"
        exit 1
      fi
    fi

    echo "Recording... Press Enter to stop, Escape to cancel"

    # Record audio using sox with a time limit trick for graceful stop
    # We'll record to a temp file and use SIGINT for proper buffer flushing
    ${pkgs.sox}/bin/rec -q -r 16000 -c 1 "$TEMP_WAV" &
    RECORD_PID=$!

    # Wait for Enter or Escape key
    while true; do
      read -r -s -n 1 key
      if [[ "$key" == "" ]]; then
        # Enter was pressed
        break
      elif [[ "$key" == $'\e' ]]; then
        # Escape was pressed - cancel recording
        echo "Cancelled"
        kill -INT $RECORD_PID 2>/dev/null || true
        wait $RECORD_PID 2>/dev/null || true

        # Restore volume if needed
        if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-original-volume ]]; then
          ORIGINAL_VOL=$(cat /tmp/dictate-original-volume)
          ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --set-volume "$ORIGINAL_VOL"
          rm -f /tmp/dictate-original-volume
        fi

        exit 1
      fi
    done

    # Keep recording for 1.5 more seconds to capture end of sentence
    echo "Finishing..."
    sleep 1.5

    # Use SIGINT (like Ctrl+C) for graceful shutdown with buffer flush
    kill -INT $RECORD_PID 2>/dev/null || true
    wait $RECORD_PID 2>/dev/null || true

    # Ensure file is fully written
    sync
    sleep 0.3

    # Restore original volume if it was lowered (for dictate-hotkey)
    if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-original-volume ]]; then
      ORIGINAL_VOL=$(cat /tmp/dictate-original-volume)
      ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --set-volume "$ORIGINAL_VOL"
      rm -f /tmp/dictate-original-volume
    fi

    ${transcribe}

    if [[ -n "$RESULT" ]]; then
      echo "$RESULT"

      # Copy to clipboard (Wayland)
      echo -n "$RESULT" | ${pkgs.wl-clipboard}/bin/wl-copy
      echo "(copied to clipboard)"
    else
      echo "No speech detected"
      exit 1
    fi
  '';

  # Script to download whisper model
  dictate-download-model = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "dictate-download-model" ''
    set -euo pipefail

    MODEL_DIR="$HOME/.local/share/whisper"
    mkdir -p "$MODEL_DIR"

    # Available models: tiny, base, small, medium, large, large-v3
    MODEL="''${1:-base}"

    # Map model names to full filenames
    case "$MODEL" in
      tiny)    FILE="ggml-tiny.bin" ;;
      base)    FILE="ggml-base.bin" ;;
      small)   FILE="ggml-small.bin" ;;
      medium)  FILE="ggml-medium.bin" ;;
      large)   FILE="ggml-large-v3.bin" ;;
      large-v3) FILE="ggml-large-v3.bin" ;;
      *)       FILE="ggml-$MODEL.bin" ;;
    esac

    OUTPUT="$MODEL_DIR/ggml-$MODEL.bin"

    echo "Downloading whisper model: $MODEL ($FILE)"
    echo "This may take a while for larger models..."

    ${pkgs.curl}/bin/curl -L --progress-bar -o "$OUTPUT" \
      "https://huggingface.co/ggerganov/whisper.cpp/resolve/main/$FILE"

    # Verify the download
    SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$OUTPUT" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    if [[ "$SIZE" -lt 1000000 ]]; then
      echo "ERROR: Download failed or file too small ($SIZE bytes)"
      echo "Contents: $(cat "$OUTPUT")"
      rm -f "$OUTPUT"
      exit 1
    fi

    echo "Model downloaded to: $OUTPUT ($SIZE bytes)"
    echo "Set WHISPER_MODEL env var or use default (base)"
  '';

  # Wrapper script that runs dictate and handles errors
  dictate-wrapper = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "dictate-wrapper" ''
    # Run dictate (volume restore happens inside dictate after recording stops)
    ${dictate}/bin/dictate
    EXIT_CODE=$?

    if [[ $EXIT_CODE -eq 0 ]]; then
      # Success - signal to hotkey script to paste by creating a temp file
      touch /tmp/dictate-success
    else
      # Error - wait for keypress before closing
      echo ""
      echo "Press any key to close..."
      read -n 1 -s
    fi
  '';

  # Hotkey script for global shortcut
  # Mutes audio, opens a floating terminal for dictation, unmutes after
  dictate-hotkey = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "dictate-hotkey" ''
    # Clean up any previous success marker
    rm -f /tmp/dictate-success

    # Save current volume and lower to 10% during dictation
    ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --get-volume > /tmp/dictate-original-volume
    ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --set-volume 10

    # Spawn a floating terminal running dictate wrapper
    ${pkgs.foot}/bin/foot \
      --title="Dictation" \
      --window-size-chars=80x15 \
      -e ${dictate-wrapper}/bin/dictate-wrapper 2>/dev/null

    # After foot closes, type the text if successful
    if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-success ]]; then
      rm -f /tmp/dictate-success
      sleep 0.2
      # Get text from clipboard and type it directly (avoids Ctrl+V issues)
      ${pkgs.wl-clipboard}/bin/wl-paste --no-newline --type text/plain 2>/dev/null | ${pkgs.wtype}/bin/wtype -d 5 - 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
  '';

in
{
  # Module options
  options.services.dictation = {
    enable = mkEnableOption "whisper-based dictation";

    model = mkOption {
      type = types.enum [
        "tiny"
        "base"
        "small"
        "medium"
        "large"
      ];
      default = "base";
      description = "Whisper model to use for transcription";
    };

    modelPath = mkOption {
      type = types.str;
      default = "$HOME/.local/share/whisper/ggml-${cfg.model}.bin";
      description = "Path to the whisper model file";
    };

    whisperPackage = mkOption {
      type = types.package;
      default = pkgs.whisper-cpp;
      description = "The whisper-cpp package to use for transcription";
    };

    server = {
      enable = mkEnableOption ''
        an always-on whisper-server that keeps the model resident in VRAM.
        Avoids per-invocation model loads (which can fail under VRAM pressure
        from e.g. ollama) and speeds up transcription'';

      host = mkOption {
        type = types.str;
        default = "127.0.0.1";
        description = "Host/IP the whisper-server listens on.";
      };

      port = mkOption {
        type = types.port;
        default = 8910;
        description = "Port the whisper-server listens on.";
      };
    };
  };

  # Module implementation
  config = mkIf cfg.enable {
    # Install dictation tools
    users.users.${username}.packages = [
      dictate
      dictate-download-model
      dictate-hotkey
      cfg.whisperPackage # Configurable whisper package
      pkgs.sox # For audio recording
      pkgs.wl-clipboard # For Wayland clipboard
      pkgs.pamixer # For volume control
      pkgs.foot # Terminal for dictation hotkey
      pkgs.wtype # For simulating keypresses (Wayland)
    ];

    # Always-on whisper-server: loads the model into VRAM once at boot and keeps
    # it resident, so dictation never races ollama for a transient allocation.
    systemd.services.whisper-server = mkIf cfg.server.enable {
      description = "whisper.cpp server (keeps the dictation model resident in VRAM)";
      wantedBy = [ "multi-user.target" ];
      after = [ "local-fs.target" ];
      serviceConfig = {
        User = username;
        SupplementaryGroups = [
          "video"
          "render"
        ];
        ExecStart = "${whisperServerBin} -m ${serverModelFile} --host ${cfg.server.host} --port ${toString cfg.server.port}";
        Restart = "on-failure";
        RestartSec = 5;
      };
    };
  };
}

lastPrompt: Currently, the dictation module inside the dev box distro has the functionality to lower the volume of the currently playing music when dictation starts. It keeps that functionality but also introduce…

content.text:
This is a custom NixOS module (`distros/devbox/dictation.nix`). The current behavior:
- `dictate-hotkey` lowers volume to 10% (saving the original to `/tmp/dictate-original-volume`)
- `dictate` restores it after recording (in both the cancel and normal-finish paths)

I'll add a `pauseMusic` feature flag (default off → current behavior) that instead pauses playback via `playerctl` and resumes it after. I'll factor the duck-start/duck-stop logic into two snippets selected by the flag, so both code paths stay in sync.

input.old_string:
RESULT=$(grep -v "^\[" "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" | tr -d '\n' | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//')
        rm -f "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" "$WHISPER_ERROR"
      '';

  # Dictation script using whisper.cpp
  dictate = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "dictate" ''
input.new_string:
RESULT=$(grep -v "^\[" "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" | tr -d '\n' | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//')
        rm -f "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" "$WHISPER_ERROR"
      '';

  # While recording we "duck" any currently-playing audio so it neither drowns
  # out the speaker nor leaks into the mic. Two strategies, selected by
  # cfg.pauseMusic:
  #   - default:    save the system volume and lower it to 10%, restoring after.
  #   - pauseMusic: pause playback (if a player is playing) and resume it after.
  # duckStart runs in dictate-hotkey; duckStop runs in dictate once recording
  # finishes or is cancelled. Each undoes only what it did.
  duckStart =
    if cfg.pauseMusic then
      ''
        # Pause playback if a player is currently playing, recording that we did
        # so duckStop only resumes what we actually paused.
        if [[ "$(${pkgs.playerctl}/bin/playerctl status 2>/dev/null)" == "Playing" ]]; then
          ${pkgs.playerctl}/bin/playerctl pause || true
          touch /tmp/dictate-paused-music
        fi
      ''
    else
      ''
        # Save current volume and lower to 10% during dictation.
        ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --get-volume > /tmp/dictate-original-volume
        ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --set-volume 10
      '';

  duckStop =
    if cfg.pauseMusic then
      ''
        # Resume playback only if we paused it.
        if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-paused-music ]]; then
          ${pkgs.playerctl}/bin/playerctl play || true
          rm -f /tmp/dictate-paused-music
        fi
      ''
    else
      ''
        # Restore the original volume if it was lowered.
        if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-original-volume ]]; then
          ORIGINAL_VOL=$(cat /tmp/dictate-original-volume)
          ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --set-volume "$ORIGINAL_VOL"
          rm -f /tmp/dictate-original-volume
        fi
      '';

  # Dictation script using whisper.cpp
  dictate = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "dictate" ''

content.text:
Done. I added a `services.dictation.pauseMusic` feature flag to `distros/devbox/dictation.nix`, defaulting **off** so current behavior is unchanged everywhere.

## What changed

**New option** (`mkEnableOption`, default `false`):
```nix
pauseMusic = mkEnableOption ''
  pausing media playback (via playerctl) while dictating and resuming it
  afterwards, instead of the default behaviour of lowering the system
  volume to 10%. Requires an MPRIS-capable player ...'';
```

**Factored the duck logic** into two flag-selected snippets so the start (in `dictate-hotkey`) and stop (both the cancel and normal-finish paths in `dictate`) stay in sync:
- `pauseMusic = false` (default) → exactly the old behavior: save volume → set to 10% → restore from `/tmp/dictate-original-volume`
- `pauseMusic = true` → if a player is `Playing`, `playerctl pause` and drop a `/tmp/dictate-paused-music` marker; on stop, `playerctl play` **only if** that marker exists (so it resumes only what it paused) and removes it

**`playerctl`** is added to the user packages only when `pauseMusic` is on (`++ optional cfg.pauseMusic pkgs.playerctl`).

## Verified
- nixfmt ✓, statix ✓, parse ✓
- lucie evaluates with default **and** with a `pauseMusic = true` override → two different system derivations, confirming the flag actually swaps behavior and pulls in `playerctl`

## To enable it
Set it on a host (e.g. `hosts/lucie/default.nix`), alongside the existing dictation config:
```nix
services.dictation.pauseMusic = true;
```

Two things worth flagging:
- **MPRIS requirement:** `playerctl` controls MPRIS players (Spotify, browsers, VLC…). Plain **mpd** doesn't expose MPRIS without a bridge (`mpd-mpris`/`mpDris2`) — if your music is mpd, tell me and I can switch the pause/resume to `mpc` instead, or make the player-control command itself an option.
- The marker-file approach means an interrupted dictation could leave music paused; the next dictation's `duckStop` would resume it. Minor, matches the existing volume-marker pattern.

Want me to commit this (and/or enable `pauseMusic` on lucie)?

message.content: What do you mean by maker file?

content.text:
A "marker file" is just a plain (usually empty) file whose **existence is used as a signal** between two separate script runs. The file's content doesn't matter — only whether it's there or not. It's a common shell trick for passing a yes/no flag between processes that can't share variables.

In this case the pause logic is split across **two separate scripts/processes**:

1. `dictate-hotkey` runs when you press the hotkey → it pauses the music and does `touch /tmp/dictate-paused-music`
2. `dictate` runs later inside the spawned terminal, finishes recording → checks `if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-paused-music ]]` and, if present, runs `playerctl play` and deletes the file

They're different processes, so they can't pass a Lua/shell variable to each other — the file on disk is how the first one tells the second one **"I paused something, so resume it."**

The reason it's a *conditional* signal rather than always resuming: it ensures we **only resume music that dictation actually paused**. If nothing was playing when you started (or you'd already paused it yourself), `duckStart` never creates the marker, so `duckStop` sees no marker and leaves playback alone instead of pressing "play" on music you didn't want resumed.

```sh
# duckStart (in dictate-hotkey): only mark if we actually paused
if [[ "$(playerctl status)" == "Playing" ]]; then
  playerctl pause
  touch /tmp/dictate-paused-music     # the marker
fi

# duckStop (in dictate): only resume what we paused
if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-paused-music ]]; then
  playerctl play
  rm -f /tmp/dictate-paused-music     # clear the marker
fi
```

This is the exact same pattern the existing code already uses — `/tmp/dictate-original-volume` is both a marker ("volume was lowered, restore it") and a value store, and `/tmp/dictate-success` is a pure marker telling the hotkey script whether to type the result. I just mirrored it for the pause/resume case.

file.content:
# While recording we "duck" any currently-playing audio so it neither drowns
  # out the speaker nor leaks into the mic. Two strategies, selected by
  # cfg.pauseMusic:
  #   - default:    save the system volume and lower it to 10%, restoring after.
  #   - pauseMusic: pause playback (if a player is playing) and resume it after.
  # duckStart runs in dictate-hotkey; duckStop runs in dictate once recording
  # finishes or is cancelled. Each undoes only what it did.
  duckStart =
    if cfg.pauseMusic then
      ''
        # Pause playback if a player is currently playing, recording that we did
        # so duckStop only resumes what we actually paused.
        if [[ "$(${pkgs.playerctl}/bin/playerctl status 2>/dev/null)" == "Playing" ]]; then
          ${pkgs.playerctl}/bin/playerctl pause || true
          touch /tmp/dictate-paused-music
        fi
      ''
    else
      ''
        # Save current volume and lower to 10% during dictation.
        ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --get-volume > /tmp/dictate-original-volume
        ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --set-volume 10
      '';

  duckStop =
    if cfg.pauseMusic then
      ''
        # Resume playback only if we paused it.
        if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-paused-music ]]; then
          ${pkgs.playerctl}/bin/playerctl play || true
          rm -f /tmp/dictate-paused-music
        fi
      ''
    else
      ''
        # Restore the original volume if it was lowered.
        if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-original-volume ]]; then
          ORIGINAL_VOL=$(cat /tmp/dictate-original-volume)
          ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --set-volume "$ORIGINAL_VOL"
          rm -f /tmp/dictate-original-volume
        fi
      '';

  # Dictation script using whisper.cpp
  dictate = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "dictate" ''

content.text:
Both branches verify and differ:
- **pauseMusic = true** (lucie's setting → waybar-mpris branch): `gzspz…` ✓
- **pauseMusic = false** (volume branch, forced): `p15cx…` ✓ — same hash as the original volume logic, confirming that path is unchanged

## What changed in `distros/devbox/dictation.nix`
The `pauseMusic` branch now uses **waybar-mpris** instead of playerctl, keeping the conditional behavior by leveraging `--send list` (which reports `Playing: true/false`):

```sh
# duckStart — pause only if something is actually playing
if waybar-mpris --send list 2>/dev/null | grep -q "Playing: true"; then
  waybar-mpris --send toggle || true
  touch /tmp/dictate-paused-music
fi

# duckStop — resume only what we paused
if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-paused-music ]]; then
  waybar-mpris --send toggle || true
  rm -f /tmp/dictate-paused-music
fi
```

Also updated: the option description, the doc comment, and the conditional package (`++ optional cfg.pauseMusic pkgs.waybar-mpris`). No `playerctl` references remain. nixfmt/statix/parse all pass.

## Two things worth knowing
- **waybar-mpris only offers `toggle`** (no explicit pause/play), so I gate it on the play state from `--send list` and rely on `--autofocus` to target the playing player. The marker file ensures one toggle to pause and one matching toggle to resume.
- **It needs the waybar-mpris daemon running** — which it is, since your waybar starts it (and your MPD is exposed via mpdris2). If dictation ever runs without waybar up, `--send` just no-ops and dictation proceeds (no pause).

I see you've already enabled it on lucie (`hosts/lucie/default.nix:104: pauseMusic = true`). Both `dictation.nix` and `lucie/default.nix` are currently modified — want me to commit them (and if so, together or separately)?

What I want to do is once the transcription is done to immediately put the entire content in the input field and minimize the chances of changing or losing focus. What can be potentially done about this?

=== dictate-hotkey: spawn + insert logic ===
  # Hotkey script for global shortcut
  # Ducks currently-playing audio (lowers volume or pauses playback), opens a
  # floating terminal for dictation, then restores audio after.
  dictate-hotkey = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "dictate-hotkey" ''
    # Clean up any previous success marker
    rm -f /tmp/dictate-success

    # Duck currently-playing audio while dictating.
    ${duckStart}

    # Spawn a floating terminal running dictate wrapper
    ${pkgs.foot}/bin/foot \
      --title="Dictation" \
      --window-size-chars=80x15 \
      -e ${dictate-wrapper}/bin/dictate-wrapper 2>/dev/null

    # After foot closes, type the text if successful
    if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-success ]]; then
      rm -f /tmp/dictate-success
      sleep 0.2
      # Get text from clipboard and type it directly (avoids Ctrl+V issues)
      ${pkgs.wl-clipboard}/bin/wl-paste --no-newline --type text/plain 2>/dev/null | ${pkgs.wtype}/bin/wtype -d 5 - 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
  '';

=== dictate-hotkey: spawn + insert logic ===
  # Hotkey script for global shortcut
  # Ducks currently-playing audio (lowers volume or pauses playback), opens a
  # floating terminal for dictation, then restores audio after.
  dictate-hotkey = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "dictate-hotkey" ''
    # Clean up any previous success marker
    rm -f /tmp/dictate-success

    # Duck currently-playing audio while dictating.
    ${duckStart}

    # Spawn a floating terminal running dictate wrapper
    ${pkgs.foot}/bin/foot \
      --title="Dictation" \
      --window-size-chars=80x15 \
      -e ${dictate-wrapper}/bin/dictate-wrapper 2>/dev/null

    # After foot closes, type the text if successful
    if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-success ]]; then
      rm -f /tmp/dictate-success
      sleep 0.2
      # Get text from clipboard and type it directly (avoids Ctrl+V issues)
      ${pkgs.wl-clipboard}/bin/wl-paste --no-newline --type text/plain 2>/dev/null | ${pkgs.wtype}/bin/wtype -d 5 - 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
  '';

lastPrompt: Currently, after the dictation stops, it starts pasting the content in the input field that's in focus, if there is such an input field. But it takes some time and if the focus changes to another inpu…

content.text:
Good diagnosis target. Here's what's happening and the realistic fixes.

## Why it splits today
`dictate-hotkey` ends with:
```sh
wl-paste --no-newline | wtype -d 5 -
```
`wtype -d 5 -` **synthesizes one keystroke per character with a 5 ms gap**. A 200-char transcription is ~1s+ of continuous fake typing. During that window anything that moves focus (the foot window closing and focus returning, a notification stealing focus, an async sway focus event, you clicking) sends the *rest* of the characters to a different field → the split you're seeing. The `sleep 0.2` is a band-aid for the foot-close → focus-return race.

So there are two separate problems: **(a) insertion isn't atomic** (slow char-by-char), and **(b) focus isn't pinned** to the target during insertion.

## Options

| # | Approach | Effect | Trade-off |
|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | **Paste via one keystroke** — text is already on the clipboard (`wl-copy`), so send a single `Ctrl+V` (`wtype -M ctrl -k v -m ctrl`) instead of typing | Whole content inserted in **one atomic op** — no typing window to split | Paste shortcut is app-specific (terminals want `Ctrl+Shift+V`). This is almost certainly the "Ctrl+V issues" your comment refers to |
| **2** | **Drop `-d 5`** (`wtype -` with no delay) | Much faster → smaller window | Still char-by-char, not atomic; fast wtype can occasionally drop/reorder chars |
| **3** | **Re-pin focus** before inserting via sway IPC: record the focused window before opening foot, `swaymsg [con_id=$ID] focus` right before paste | Guarantees the right window is focused at insert time; kills the foot-close race | sway-specific (fine for you), needs `jq`+`swaymsg`; pair with #1/#2 |
| **4** | **Record without stealing focus** — no foot window; background recorder, toggle-hotkey to stop, feedback via notification/waybar | Focus never leaves the field → problem gone at the root | Bigger UX rework (lose the terminal "Recording…" UI and Enter-to-stop) |

## Recommendation
**#1 + #3 together.** Replace the char-by-char typing with a single `Ctrl+V` (atomic — exactly your "put the entire content in immediately"), and pin focus back to the originally-focused window before pasting so it can't drift during the foot-close handoff. The text stays on the clipboard either way, so a misfire is recoverable with a manual paste.

The one wrinkle is #1's app-specific shortcut. Two ways to handle it:
- **Detect the focused app** via `swaymsg -t get_tree` (focused `app_id`/`class`) and send `Ctrl+Shift+V` for terminals, `Ctrl+V` otherwise.
- Or just use `Ctrl+V` if your dictation targets are GUI/browser input fields (the common case) — simplest and reliable there.

If you want the absolute minimum change first, **#2** (drop `-d 5`) is a one-liner that noticeably shrinks the window — but it's mitigation, not a cure; #1 is the real fix.

Want me to implement #1 + #3 (with focus-restore and app-aware paste shortcut), or start with the quick #2 change?

content.text:
Confirmed clean — that `1` was `grep`'s exit (it had nothing to print), not statix. Real statix exit is **0**. All checks pass: nixfmt ✓, statix ✓, parse ✓, lucie evaluates ✓.

## Change (`distros/devbox/dictation.nix`, in `dictate-hotkey`)
```sh
# before: typed char-by-char (slow, splittable)
wl-paste --no-newline | wtype -d 5 -

# after: one atomic paste of the whole clipboard
wtype -M ctrl -k v -m ctrl
```
The transcription is already on the clipboard (`wl-copy` in `dictate`), so this sends a single `Ctrl+V` — the entire content lands in one action, eliminating the multi-second typing window that was causing the split.

## To try it
`nixos-rebuild switch` on lucie, then dictate into a **GUI/browser input field**.

## Expected caveat (by design of #1)
`Ctrl+V` works in browsers and most GUI apps, but **not terminals** — those need `Ctrl+Shift+V`, so pasting into a terminal won't work with this version. If you hit that, the follow-up is the app-aware variant (detect the focused `app_id` via `swaymsg` and send the right shortcut) plus the focus-restore from #3. The text remains on the clipboard regardless, so a manual paste is always a fallback.

=== man page ===
.\" Manpage for wtype.
.TH wtype 1 "23 November 2020" "0.4" "wtype man page"
.SH NAME
wtype \- xdotool type for Wayland
.SH SYNOPSIS
.B wtype
[TEXT]

.B wtype
[OPTION_OR_TEXT]... -- [TEXT]...

.B wtype
[OPTION_OR_TEXT] -

.SH DESCRIPTION
.PP
wtype is a Wayland tool that allows you to simulate keyboard input like xdotool type for X11.
This is accomplished via the virtual-keyboard Wayland protocol.

.SH OPTIONS
Modifier can be one of "shift", "capslock", "ctrl", "logo", "win", "alt", "altgr".
Beware that the modifiers get released automatically once the program terminates.

Named keys are resolved by libxkbcommon, valid identifiers include "Left" and
"Home".

.TP
.BR \-M\ \fIMOD\fR
Press modifier MOD.

.TP
.BR \-m\ \fIMOD\fR
Release modifier MOD.

.TP
.BR \-P\ \fIKEY\fR
Press key KEY.

.TP
.BR \-p\ \fIKEY\fR
Release key KEY.

.TP
.BR \-k\ \fIKEY\fR
Type (press and release) key KEY.

.TP
.BR \-d\ \fITIME\fR
Sleep for TIME milliseconds between keystrokes when typing texts.
Can be used multiple times, default 0.
.TP

.BR \-s\ \fITIME\fR
Sleep for TIME milliseconds before interpreting the following options. This
can be used to perform more complicated modifier sequences.

.TP
.BR \-
Read text to type from stdin. This option can appear only once.

.SH BUGS
No known bugs.

.SH AUTHOR
wtype has been started by Josef Gajdusek.
Man page is written by Michael 'jubalh' Vetter.

content.text: Found the fix. wtype's man page explicitly documents `-s TIME` as: *"Sleep for TIME ms before interpreting the following options. This can be used to perform more complicated modifier sequences."* Your `-M ctrl -k v -m ctrl` fires all events instantly, so the app often doesn't see Ctrl as *held* when V arrives — nothing pastes. Spacing the sequence with `-s` is the documented way to make combos register.

content.text:
All checks pass (nixfmt, statix, parse, lucie evaluates).

## The fix
```sh
# before — events fire instantly, app misses "Ctrl held" when V arrives → no paste
wtype -M ctrl -k v -m ctrl

# after — -s spaces out the sequence so Ctrl is registered as held during V
wtype -M ctrl -s 60 -k v -s 60 -m ctrl
```
This holds Ctrl, waits 60 ms, taps V, waits 60 ms, releases Ctrl (~120 ms total, imperceptible). Per wtype's man page, `-s` exists specifically "to perform more complicated modifier sequences" — your instant combo was the reason nothing pasted while a real Ctrl+V worked.

## To test
`nixos-rebuild switch` on lucie, then dictate into a **GUI/browser** input field — it should auto-paste now.

A couple of notes:
- If 60 ms still occasionally misses, bump both `-s` values (e.g. 100–150); some apps need a longer hold.
- The terminal caveat stands: this sends `Ctrl+V`, which terminals don't accept (they want `Ctrl+Shift+V`). If you dictate into terminals too, the follow-up is the app-aware variant (detect focused `app_id` via `swaymsg` → pick the shortcut), optionally with focus-restore.

content.text: Your terminals are **kitty** (default) and **alacritty**, plus the dictation window is **foot**. I'll capture the focused window's `app_id` *before* foot steals focus, then choose `Ctrl+Shift+V` for terminals and `Ctrl+V` otherwise. Using `swaymsg`/`jq` (both available).

input.old_string:
# Clean up any previous success marker
    rm -f /tmp/dictate-success

    # Duck currently-playing audio while dictating.
    ${duckStart}
input.new_string:
# Clean up any previous success marker
    rm -f /tmp/dictate-success

    # Record the focused window's app id *before* the dictation terminal steals
    # focus, so we can pick the right paste shortcut afterwards (terminals paste
    # with Ctrl+Shift+V, other apps with Ctrl+V). app_id covers native Wayland
    # windows; window_properties.class covers XWayland ones. Empty on failure,
    # which falls through to the Ctrl+V default below.
    FOCUSED_APP=$(${pkgs.sway}/bin/swaymsg -t get_tree 2>/dev/null \
      | ${pkgs.jq}/bin/jq -r 'recurse(.nodes[]?, .floating_nodes[]?) | select(.focused == true) | (.app_id // .window_properties.class // "")' \
      | head -n1)

input.old_string:
# After foot closes, paste the text if successful
    if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-success ]]; then
      rm -f /tmp/dictate-success
      sleep 0.2
      # The transcription is already on the clipboard (wl-copy in dictate). Paste
      # it with a single Ctrl+V keystroke instead of typing it out character by
      # character: the whole content lands in one atomic action, so it can't be
      # split across fields if focus drifts mid-insert.
      #
      # The -s delays space out the modifier sequence (press Ctrl, hold, tap V,
      # hold, release Ctrl). Without them wtype fires the events instantly and the
      # target app often doesn't register Ctrl as held when V arrives, so nothing
      # pastes (the man page recommends -s for exactly these modifier sequences).
      # NOTE: Ctrl+V works in most GUI/browser input fields but not terminals
      # (they want Ctrl+Shift+V).
      ${pkgs.wtype}/bin/wtype -M ctrl -s 60 -k v -s 60 -m ctrl 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
input.new_string:
# After foot closes, paste the text if successful
    if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-success ]]; then
      rm -f /tmp/dictate-success
      sleep 0.2
      # The transcription is already on the clipboard (wl-copy in dictate). Paste
      # it with a single keystroke instead of typing it out character by
      # character: the whole content lands in one atomic action, so it can't be
      # split across fields if focus drifts mid-insert.
      #
      # The -s delays space out the modifier sequence so the target app registers
      # the modifiers as held when V arrives; without them wtype fires the events
      # instantly and nothing pastes (the man page recommends -s for exactly these
      # modifier sequences). Terminals paste with Ctrl+Shift+V, everything else
      # (GUI/browser input fields) with Ctrl+V.
      case "''${FOCUSED_APP,,}" in
        kitty | alacritty | foot | xterm | *wezterm* | *ghostty*)
          ${pkgs.wtype}/bin/wtype -M ctrl -M shift -s 60 -k v -s 60 -m shift -m ctrl 2>/dev/null || true
          ;;
        *)
          ${pkgs.wtype}/bin/wtype -M ctrl -s 60 -k v -s 60 -m ctrl 2>/dev/null || true
          ;;
      esac
    fi

content.text:
Works. Live test returned `slack` (a GUI app → correctly falls to the `Ctrl+V` branch), confirming the `swaymsg`/`jq` pipeline detects the focused app. nixfmt ✓, statix ✓, parse ✓, lucie evaluates ✓.

## What it does now (`dictate-hotkey`)
1. **Before** opening the foot dictation window, captures the focused window's `app_id` (or XWayland `class`):
   ```sh
   FOCUSED_APP=$(swaymsg -t get_tree | jq -r '… select(.focused==true) | (.app_id // .window_properties.class // "")' | head -n1)
   ```
2. **After** dictation, picks the paste shortcut by app (case-insensitive):
   ```sh
   case "${FOCUSED_APP,,}" in
     kitty | alacritty | foot | xterm | *wezterm* | *ghostty*)
       wtype -M ctrl -M shift -s 60 -k v -s 60 -m shift -m ctrl ;;   # Ctrl+Shift+V
     *)
       wtype -M ctrl -s 60 -k v -s 60 -m ctrl ;;                     # Ctrl+V
   esac
   ```

Capturing before foot means `FOCUSED_APP` is your real target (kitty, slack, browser…), not the foot window. If detection ever fails (no `SWAYSOCK`), `FOCUSED_APP` is empty → defaults to `Ctrl+V`.

## To test
`nixos-rebuild switch` on lucie, then dictate into:
- **kitty/alacritty** → should auto-paste via Ctrl+Shift+V
- **browser/Slack/GUI field** → auto-paste via Ctrl+V

If you use another terminal I didn't list, tell me its `app_id` (run the `swaymsg … jq` line while it's focused) and I'll add it.

Want me to commit this?

1	# Speech-to-text dictation using whisper.cpp
2	# Usage: dictate (records audio, transcribes with whisper, types result)
3	{
4	  config,
5	  pkgs,
6	  lib,
7	  username,
8	  ...
9	}:
10	
11	with lib;
12	
13	let
14	  cfg = config.services.dictation;
15	
16	  # The whisper binary to use
17	  whisperBin = "${cfg.whisperPackage}/bin/whisper-cli";
18	  whisperServerBin = "${cfg.whisperPackage}/bin/whisper-server";
19	
20	  serverUrl = "http://${cfg.server.host}:${toString cfg.server.port}";
21	
22	  # Absolute model path for the system service ($HOME is not expanded in ExecStart).
23	  serverModelFile = "${
24	    config.users.users.${username}.home
25	  }/.local/share/whisper/ggml-${cfg.model}.bin";
26	
27	  # Transcription step. Sets $RESULT to the trimmed transcription, or exits 1.
28	  # Server mode posts the wav to the always-on whisper-server (model stays
29	  # resident in VRAM); otherwise a one-shot whisper-cli loads the model each run.
30	  transcribe =
31	    if cfg.server.enable then
32	      ''
33	        echo "Transcribing (server)..."
34	
35	        WHISPER_OUTPUT=$(mktemp /tmp/whisper-output-XXXXXX.txt)
36	        WHISPER_ERROR=$(mktemp /tmp/whisper-error-XXXXXX.txt)
37	
38	        HTTP_CODE=$(${pkgs.curl}/bin/curl -s -o "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" -w "%{http_code}" \
39	          --max-time 60 \
40	          -F file=@"$TEMP_WAV" \
41	          -F temperature=0 \
42	          -F response_format=text \
43	          "${serverUrl}/inference" 2>"$WHISPER_ERROR") || true
44	
45	        if [[ "$HTTP_CODE" != "200" ]]; then
46	          echo "ERROR: whisper-server request failed (HTTP ''${HTTP_CODE:-none})!"
47	          echo "Is it running?  systemctl status whisper-server"
48	          echo ""
49	          cat "$WHISPER_ERROR" "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" 2>/dev/null || true
50	          rm -f "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" "$WHISPER_ERROR"
51	          exit 1
52	        fi
53	
54	        RESULT=$(tr -d '\n' < "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//')
55	        rm -f "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" "$WHISPER_ERROR"
56	      ''
57	    else
58	      ''
59	        echo "Transcribing..."
60	
61	        # Run whisper and capture both stdout and stderr
62	        WHISPER_OUTPUT=$(mktemp /tmp/whisper-output-XXXXXX.txt)
63	        WHISPER_ERROR=$(mktemp /tmp/whisper-error-XXXXXX.txt)
64	
65	        if ! ${whisperBin} \
66	          -m "$WHISPER_MODEL" \
67	          -f "$TEMP_WAV" \
68	          -nt \
69	          >"$WHISPER_OUTPUT" 2>"$WHISPER_ERROR"; then
70	
71	          echo "ERROR: Whisper transcription failed!"
72	          echo ""
73	          cat "$WHISPER_ERROR"
74	
75	          rm -f "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" "$WHISPER_ERROR"
76	          exit 1
77	        fi
78	
79	        RESULT=$(grep -v "^\[" "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" | tr -d '\n' | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//')
80	        rm -f "$WHISPER_OUTPUT" "$WHISPER_ERROR"
81	      '';
82	
83	  # While recording we "duck" any currently-playing audio so it neither drowns
84	  # out the speaker nor leaks into the mic. Two strategies, selected by
85	  # cfg.pauseMusic:
86	  #   - default:    save the system volume and lower it to 10%, restoring after.
87	  #   - pauseMusic: toggle playback via waybar-mpris (if a player is playing)
88	  #                 and toggle it back after.
89	  # duckStart runs in dictate-hotkey; duckStop runs in dictate once recording
90	  # finishes or is cancelled. Each undoes only what it did.
91	  duckStart =
92	    if cfg.pauseMusic then
93	      ''
94	        # waybar-mpris only offers a toggle, so gate it on the play state from
95	        # `--send list`: toggle (pause) only if something is currently playing,
96	        # recording that we did so duckStop only resumes what we paused.
97	        # --autofocus makes the toggle target the playing player.
98	        if ${pkgs.waybar-mpris}/bin/waybar-mpris --send list 2>/dev/null | grep -q "Playing: true"; then
99	          ${pkgs.waybar-mpris}/bin/waybar-mpris --send toggle || true
100	          touch /tmp/dictate-paused-music
101	        fi
102	      ''
103	    else
104	      ''
105	        # Save current volume and lower to 10% during dictation.
106	        ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --get-volume > /tmp/dictate-original-volume
107	        ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --set-volume 10
108	      '';
109	
110	  duckStop =
111	    if cfg.pauseMusic then
112	      ''
113	        # Resume (toggle back) playback only if we paused it.
114	        if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-paused-music ]]; then
115	          ${pkgs.waybar-mpris}/bin/waybar-mpris --send toggle || true
116	          rm -f /tmp/dictate-paused-music
117	        fi
118	      ''
119	    else
120	      ''
121	        # Restore the original volume if it was lowered.
122	        if [[ -f /tmp/dictate-original-volume ]]; then
123	          ORIGINAL_VOL=$(cat /tmp/dictate-original-volume)
124	          ${pkgs.pamixer}/bin/pamixer --set-volume "$ORIGINAL_VOL"
125	          rm -f /tmp/dictate-original-volume
126	        fi
127	      '';
128	
129	  # Dictation script using whisper.cpp
130	  dictate = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "dictate" ''
131	    set -euo pipefail
132	
133	    DEFAULT_MODEL="${cfg.model}"
134	    DEFAULT_MODEL_PATH="${cfg.modelPath}"
135	    WHISPER_MODEL="''${WHISPER_MODEL:-$DEFAULT_MODEL_PATH}"
136	    TEMP_WAV=$(mktemp /tmp/dictation-XXXXXX.wav)
137	
138	    cleanup() {
139	      rm -f "$TEMP_WAV"
140	    }
141	    trap cleanup EXIT
142	
143	    # Check if model exists, offer to download if not
144	    if [[ ! -f "$WHISPER_MODEL" ]]; then
145	      echo "Whisper model not found at: $WHISPER_MODEL"
146	      echo ""
147	      read -p "Would you like to download the '$DEFAULT_MODEL' model? [y/N] " -n 1 -r
148	      echo ""
149	
150	      if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
151	        echo "Downloading $DEFAULT_MODEL model..."
152	        ${dictate-download-model}/bin/dictate-download-model "$DEFAULT_MODEL"
153	        echo ""
154	        echo "Model downloaded. Starting dictation..."
155	        echo ""
156	      else
157	        echo "Aborted. You can manually download with: dictate-download-model $DEFAULT_MODEL"
158	        exit 1
159	      fi
160	    fi
161	
162	    echo "Recording... Press Enter to stop, Escape to cancel"
163	
164	    # Record audio using sox with a time limit trick for graceful stop
165	    # We'll record to a temp file and use SIGINT for proper buffer flushing
166	    ${pkgs.sox}/bin/rec -q -r 16000 -c 1 "$TEMP_WAV" &
167	    RECORD_PID=$!
168	
169	    # Wait for Enter or Escape key
170	    while true; do
171	      read -r -s -n 1 key
172	      if [[ "$key" == "" ]]; then
173	        # Enter was pressed
174	        break
175	      elif [[ "$key" == $'\e' ]]; then
176	        # Escape was pressed - cancel recording
177	        echo "Cancelled"
178	        kill -INT $RECORD_PID 2>/dev/null || true
179	        wait $RECORD_PID 2>/dev/null || true
180	
181	        # Undo audio ducking on cancel
182	        ${duckStop}
183	
184	        exit 1
185	      fi
186	    done
187	
188	    # Keep recording for 1.5 more seconds to capture end of sentence
189	    echo "Finishing..."
190	    sleep 1.5
191	
192	    # Use SIGINT (like Ctrl+C) for graceful shutdown with buffer flush
193	    kill -INT $RECORD_PID 2>/dev/null || true
194	    wait $RECORD_PID 2>/dev/null || true
195	
196	    # Ensure file is fully written
197	    sync
198	    sleep 0.3
199	
200	    # Undo audio ducking after recording stops (for dictate-hotkey)
201	    ${duckStop}
202	
203	    ${transcribe}
204	
205	    if [[ -n "$RESULT" ]]; then
206	      echo "$RESULT"
207	
208	      # Copy to clipboard (Wayland)
209	      echo -n "$RESULT" | ${pkgs.wl-clipboard}/bin/wl-copy
210	      echo "(copied to clipboard)"
211	    else
212	      echo "No speech detected"
213	      exit 1
214	    fi
215	  '';
216	
217	  # Script to download whisper model
218	  dictate-download-model = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "dictate-download-model" ''
219	    set -euo pipefail
220	
221	    MODEL_DIR="$HOME/.local/share/whisper"
222	    mkdir -p "$MODEL_DIR"
223	
224	    # Available models: tiny, base, small, medium, large, large-v3
225	    MODEL="''${1:-base}"
226	
227	    # Map model names to full filenames
228	    case "$MODEL" in

+      # the modifiers as held when V arrives; without them wtype fires the events
+      # instantly and nothing pastes (the man page recommends -s for exactly these
+      # modifier sequences). Terminals paste with Ctrl+Shift+V, everything else
+      # (GUI/browser input fields) with Ctrl+V.
+      case "''${FOCUSED_APP,,}" in
+        kitty | alacritty | foot | xterm | *terminal* )
+          ${pkgs.wtype}/bin/wtype -M ctrl -M shift -s 60 -k v -s 60 -m shift -m ctrl 2>/dev/null || true
+          ;;
+        *)
+          ${pkgs.wtype}/bin/wtype -M ctrl -s 60 -k v -s 60 -m ctrl 2>/dev/null || true
+          ;;
+      esac
     fi
   '';

content.text:
Committed. Parse, nixfmt (local + pre-commit hook) all passed.

**Commit `4ed0e8e`** — `dictation: auto-paste with terminal-aware shortcut (Ctrl+Shift+V vs Ctrl+V)` (1 file, +28/−3):
- Captures focused `app_id`/`class` via `swaymsg`/`jq` before foot steals focus
- Pastes with `Ctrl+Shift+V` for terminals (`kitty | alacritty | foot | xterm | *terminal*`), `Ctrl+V` otherwise
- `-s` delays space out the modifier sequence so the combo registers

The hand-tweaked `*terminal*` catch-all is preserved (it'll match generic terminal app_ids like `org.gnome.Terminal`, `xfce4-terminal`, etc.). The unrelated `.entire/.gitignore`, `.claude/`, `.opencode/` remain uncommitted.

`nixos-rebuild switch` on lucie, then test dictation into kitty/alacritty (Ctrl+Shift+V path) and a browser/GUI field (Ctrl+V path).
